name: El Castillo de las Guardas - Las Cañadillas Trail
name_original: Del Castillo de las Guardas a Las Cañadillas — circular por Sierra Bermeja
description: Hike the moderate 7.0-mile El Castillo de las Guardas - Las Cañadillas Trail through dehesas and past historical ruins in 3 hours.
region_intro: Hiking around El Castillo De Las Guardas offers diverse natural landscapes in the northwest of Seville province. The region is characterized by extensive *dehesas* with holm oaks and cork oaks, and is bisected by the Guadiamar River, which provides lush gallery forests. Trails often feature views of hills and historical ruins, including the 13th-14th century Castillo de las Guardas. Best hiking trails around El Castillo De Las Guardas The most popular hiking route is El Castillo de las Guardas - Las Cañadillas Trail, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route passes through dehesas and historical ruins. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Ronquillo Greenway, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.5 km) path. This trail runs alongside the scenic La Minilla Reservoir. Local hikers also love the Path Between Stone Walls – Scenic Rocky Path loop from El Castillo de las Guardas, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through traditional dehesas and stone walls, often completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around El Castillo De Las Guardas is defined by *dehesas*, the Guadiamar River, and historical ruins. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in El Castillo De Las Guardas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 380 hikers have used komoot to explore El Castillo De Las Guardas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Castillo de las Guardas?
A: El Castillo de las Guardas offers a wide network of trails, with over 20 routes available for hiking. These include options for various fitness levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in El Castillo de las Guardas?
A: The region is characterized by picturesque &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; with holm oaks and cork oaks, and is bisected by the Guadiamar River, which provides lush gallery forests. You'll also encounter views of mountains, historical ruins, and the scents of aromatic plants like rosemary and lavender. Some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010352668/el-ronquillo-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Ronquillo Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, run alongside the scenic La Minilla Reservoir.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in El Castillo de las Guardas?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38140809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir (south) loop from El Menor&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.8 km route that takes just under 1.5 hours to complete, offering gentle terrain around the reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in El Castillo de las Guardas?
A: Yes, many trails in the area are circular. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Between Stone Walls – Scenic Rocky Path loop from El Castillo de las Guardas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.3 km long and takes about 2 hours 22 minutes, leading through traditional dehesas and stone walls.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can explore historical ruins, including those of the 13th-14th century Castillo de las Guardas itself. Natural attractions include the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the diverse landscapes of the &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4402997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Castillo de las Guardas?
A: The region is appealing for outdoor activities year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Even in summer, the lush gallery forests along the Guadiamar River and the shade from the holm oaks in the &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; can provide relief. Winter hikes can also be enjoyable, offering a different perspective of the natural beauty.

Q: Are there any trails with good viewpoints in El Castillo de las Guardas?
A: Yes, many trails offer views of the surrounding mountains and landscapes. The Cerro del Castillo Viewpoint provides breathtaking panoramas of the area. Some routes also offer views of reservoirs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3519784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Agrio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in El Castillo de las Guardas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the tranquility of the &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, and the historical elements found along the paths.

Q: Are there any specific trails that pass by the Castillo de las Guardas ruins?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010559049/el-castillo-de-las-guardas-las-canadillas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castillo de las Guardas - Las Cañadillas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 11.2 km route that passes through &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; and historical ruins, including those of the castle.

Q: Is there a wildlife park or nature reserve nearby that can be combined with a hike?
A: Yes, the El Castillo de las Guardas Nature Reserve is located near the town's old mine. It's one of Europe's largest wildlife parks, housing over 100 species in semi-open enclosures. While not a hiking trail itself, it offers a unique experience that can complement a hiking trip to the area.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Guadiamar River?
A: The region is bisected by the Guadiamar River, and some routes, like the El Guadiamar Circular Trail (also known as Las Cañadillas Trail), follow its course, offering lush gallery forests and scenic views.
tips: The town of Castillo de las Guardas has outstanding buildings such as the Church of San Juan Bautista, in the Mudejar style or the Castle, created at the time of the reconquest, from where you can see the Guadiamar river, its banks and part of the vast area of this municipality.
Small low mountain town that is part of the province of Seville and that is at the foot of the Sierra de Bermeja. The town is crossed by the Río Guadiamar, located to the northeast of the town!
Rural path that runs through the forests of the Sierra de Bermeja, among local species such as mainly oaks, located between dry stone walls.

Public road that goes in the direction of the small town located to the north of the Sierra de Bermeja and called Las Cañadillas. The path is between private properties and where, at times, you will have to close the doors that open as you pass.
Path that reaches the town of El Castillo de las Guardas and where we find different animals in a natural environment!

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the El Castillo de las Guardas - Las Cañadillas Trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the villages of El Castillo de las Guardas or Las Cañadillas, which serve as common starting points for this circular route. Some variations might also start from Arroyo de la Plata.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as you'll pass through pastureland with grazing cattle and potentially free-ranging pigs, it's advisable to keep your dog on a leash to ensure safety for both your pet and the livestock.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is best enjoyed outside of periods of heavy rain, as crossing the numerous streams can become significantly challenging. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Avoid the peak heat of summer.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It follows public paths and is part of a network approved by the Andalusian Mountaineering Federation.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily traverses wide natural paths, including sections through dehesas (oak meadows) and old paved lanes. You'll encounter a serpentine route crossing several hills and streams, with some parts featuring stone walls and cattle gates. There can be rocky sections and uneven surfaces due to vehicle passage.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenery along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience picturesque dehesas with holm oaks and cork oaks, beautiful gallery forests, and views of mountains. Keep an eye out for the ruins of the 13th-14th century Castillo de las Guardas, often visible from a distance. The tranquil village of El Castillo de las Guardas and the charming Las Cañadillas are also notable. You'll also follow a Path Between Stone Walls and a Public Path to Las Cañadillas.

faqs.question: How difficult is the El Castillo de las Guardas - Las Cañadillas Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally rated as moderate, suitable for those with good physical condition and steady footing. While mostly easy-going, it includes some ascents and descents, such as a notable 1 km uphill climb to reach El Castillo de las Guardas from Barranco de Cagancho.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna can be seen on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in biodiversity. You'll see holm oaks, cork oaks, rockroses, gorse, and aromatic plants like rosemary and lavender. The area is known for its abundance of palm hearts and prominent bird song. You might also spot grazing cattle, horses, and pigs in the dehesa landscape.

faqs.question: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area is rich in history. Besides the ancient Castillo de las Guardas, you might encounter remnants like the Cotolío mill, an important technological site. The region also has historical remains such as dolmens and tombs in the surrounding areas.

faqs.question: What should I consider bringing or preparing for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for rocky sections, sturdy hiking footwear is recommended. Bring sufficient water, especially on warmer days, as there's no mention of water sources directly on the trail. Be prepared for sun exposure in the open dehesa areas.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Castillo de las Guardas - Las Cañadillas Trail is a circular route, allowing you to start and end in the same location, typically one of the villages like El Castillo de las Guardas or Las Cañadillas.

waypoints.name: Dehesa Landscape with Horses and Pigs
